content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments are confirmed as real bugs by code inspection. The seen_ids dedup silently drops acceptance criteria when a bead has dual roles (verified at lines 1304-1398). The unconditional load_bead_summaries call is a genuine regression for the --prompt-file path (verified at lines 310-329 and service.rs:400-405). The heading filter's hash_count < 2 check causes level-1 headings to emit verbatim as summaries (verified at lines 1069-1075).

## Votes

- `fr-10-eb47f625`: Accept
  Verified. build_planned_elsewhere_context at line 1304 initializes a seen_ids BTreeSet. Dependents are added first (lines 1353-1370), and when the shared_acceptance_owners loop (lines 1372-1398) encounters the same bead ID, seen_ids.insert() returns false and the entire PlannedElsewherePromptContext—including the criteria_label with acceptance criterion IDs—is silently dropped. This is a real information-loss bug: a bead that is both a downstream dependent and a shared acceptance owner loses its acceptance criteria relationship in the prompt. Either merging the relationship metadata or explicitly filtering and documenting precedence would fix it.
- `fr-10-265dc6c6`: Accept
  Verified. handle_create_from_bead unconditionally calls load_bead_summaries() at line 321, which runs br list and can fail with CorruptRecord (hard error at line 323) or propagate through ensure_direct_relations_have_statuses_for_prompt (line 325). Meanwhile, create_project_from_bead_context at service.rs:400-405 discards all that enriched context when prompt_override is Some. This is a genuine regression: --prompt-file users hit failures from dependency-resolution machinery that is irrelevant to their override prompt. Short-circuiting the summary loading when an override is present is the correct fix.
- `fr-10-88fbd81a`: Accept
  Verified. markdown_heading_title at line 1072 requires hash_count >= 2, so a valid level-1 heading (# Heading) returns None. In compact_planned_elsewhere_summary, that line bypasses the heading skip at line 1153, doesn't match scope labels or list markers, and falls through to the verbatim fallback at line 1170. The result is Summary: # Heading in the prompt instead of actual content. Expanding the heading check to skip any ATX heading level (hash_count >= 1) for this compaction path is a correct, low-risk fix.
